title: How to import OAI-PMH identifiers in OpenRefine
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"52lge","text":"Protocollo per importare in OpenRefine una lista di identificatori OAI-PMH per successive elaborazioni.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3nksi","text":"Installare il software OpenRefine sulla propria postazione di lavoro.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Open OpenRefine and choose "Create project". OpenRefine https://github.com/OpenRefine/OpenRefine Freebase, then Google, now open source community

Select "Web Addresses (URLs)" as the data source for the new project and click "Next". Enter the OAI-PMH baseurl of the identified target, choose the ListIdentifiers verb and the appropriate metadataPrefix. E.g. http://www.bibliotecheoggi.it/trends/oai?verb=ListIdentifiers&metadataPrefix=oai_dc The OAI-PMH protocol allows per-date and per-set limiters to be set to expose partial datasets and allow incremental collections. Es. http://an.oa.org/OAI-script?verb=ListIdentifiers&from=1998-01-15&metadataPrefix=oldArXiv&set=physics:hep
Select "XML Files" in the "Parse Data As" section. Screenshot of import options in OpenRefine
In the preview area of the file to be imported, locate the first line containing the element and bring the mouse pointer to highlight it. Then click on it. Highlight the identifier
At that point you will have obtained the list of OAI-PMH identifiers, one per line, ready for further processing.
creator: Andrea Marchitelli
